title: Puffed Pastry Australian Hake En-Croute
description: Buttery, aerated pastry filled with tender Australian hake, perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Place the hake fillets on one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around them.
Sprinkle the chopped herbs and grated cheese over the fish.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water, then fold the other half over the fish to enclose.
Crimp the edges to seal, and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Brush the pastry with a little milk, then sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Place the pastry on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the pastry from the oven, and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
To make the beurre manié, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of all-purpose flour to the butter, and whisk until smooth.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the mixture is lightly golden and has a nutty aroma.
Stir in 1/4 cup of milk, and bring to a simmer.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Season with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the beurre manié over the top of the pastry, and enjoy!
